profile.behavior: Usually moves alone or in pairs through low, tangled cover, hopping, creeping, and probing bark, leaf litter, and crevices for invertebrates; often holds the tail cocked while foraging and gives loud, repeated whistled songs.
field_marks: Small, compact wren with a round body and large-headed look
Warm buffy-orange underparts with a clean white throat and chin
Long, bold white eyebrow stripe above a dark eye
Long, slender, slightly downcurved dark bill
Long tail, often cocked upward; wings and tail show dark barring
profile.habitat: Dense, brushy cover in open woodland, forest understory and edges, thickets, ravines, overgrown farmland, gardens, and shrubby residential areas.

sources.title: Carolina Wren Identification — Cornell Lab of Ornithology
Carolina Wren Life History — Cornell Lab of Ornithology

findings: Independent verification confirms the identity, scientific name, family, stated measurements, plumage, long white eyebrow, white throat/chin, dark slender downcurved bill, barred wings and tail, cocked tail posture, behavior, and habitat are consistent with Carolina Wren.
The bird has one head, one beak, two wings, two legs, and one tail. The far wing is partly occluded but anatomically implied; no duplicated or missing structures are present.

Minor illustration issue: the visible tarsi, toes, and claws are slightly oversized and more elongated than in the supplied reference photos; this is stylistic rather than a material species or anatomy error.
No place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears on the candidate plate.
